diagnostics.subj: [dlnraja/com.tuya.zigbee] Weekly Fingerprint Sync (8 new lines) (PR #160)

ghInfo.author: github-actions[bot]
ghInfo.bodyExcerpt:
## Automated Weekly Fingerprint Sync

This PR was auto-generated by the weekly fingerprint sync workflow.

### What changed
- Fetched latest fingerprints from Z2M (tuya, sonoff, danfoss, schneider, legrand, bosch, ikea, xiaomi)
- Auto-added safe Tuya _T* and SONOFF/eWeLink (SNZB-*, ZBMINI*, S31ZB, TRVZB, SWV-*) patterns to appropriate drivers
- All JSON validated

### Reports
See data/community-sync/sync-report.md for full details.
See .github/state/johan-full-scan.json for Johan code

diagnostics.subj: [dlnraja/com.tuya.zigbee] Fix presence_sensor_radar pairing for DP-only TS0601 devices (PR #180)

ghInfo.author: Robsta
ghInfo.bodyExcerpt:
## Summary

- TS0601 devices using Tuya's DP protocol (`0xEF00`) reject standard ZCL bindings for clusters 1, 1280, and 1030 with `zdoInvalidEndpoint`, causing `create_bindings_failed` during pairing
- Changed bindings from `[1, 1280, 1030]` to `[61184]` (the Tuya DP cluster the devices actually support)

## Test plan

- [x] Tested with `_TZE204_sxm7l9xa` â pairing now succeeds (previously failed with `create_bindings_failed`)
- [x] Tested with `_TZE204_qasjif9e` â pairing now su

I've logged these for the next release. If you can share a device interview from [Developer Tools](https://tools.developer.homey.app) and a Z2M link (if it exists), that helps a lot.

Thanks for this PR! The fingerprints have been tracked and will be reviewed.

diagnostics.subj: Re: [dlnraja/com.tuya.zigbee] Fix presence_sensor_radar pairing for DP-only TS0601 devices (PR #180)

ghInfo.author: Robsta
ghInfo.bodyExcerpt:
Robsta86 left a comment (dlnraja/com.tuya.zigbee#180)

Both `_TZE204_sxm7l9xa` and `_TZE204_qasjif9e` are already in the `presence_sensor_radar` driver's manufacturer list â this PR doesn't add new fingerprints, it fixes a pairing failure for existing ones.

The issue is that these TS0601 devices only have input clusters `[0, 4, 5, 61184]` and reject bindings for clusters 1, 1280, and 1030 with `zdoInvalidEndpoint`. The fix changes bindings from `[1, 1280, 1030]` to `[61184]`.

### Devic
